subtitle: SAFe · Scaled Delivery · Agile Coaching
hero_sub: Ten years operating at the intersection of servant leadership, program-level coordination, and organizational transformation. Not one or two — all three.

the_case.need: 10+ years as Scrum Master, Agile Coach, or Program Manager
the_case.answer: CSM certified (current). 10+ years documented across SM, Agile Coach, and Program Manager roles. First SM certification in 2014 — continuous practice since.
the_case.detail: The 10-year mark matters because it separates people who have run ceremonies from people who have changed organizations. I've done both — and the organizational change work is where the career value is.

the_case.need: Experience in scaled Agile environments — SAFe, NEXUS, LeSS
the_case.answer: SAFe delivery at Eptura (PI Planning, ART coordination, ~10 distributed teams) and DigitasLBI/SunTrust (SAFe, PI-aligned, regulated financial services). NEXUS Scrum coaching at Paperless Post.
the_case.detail: I've operated in SAFe environments at enterprise scale across two distinct contexts: a commercial SaaS PMO and a regulated financial-services program. PI Planning, ART syncs, System Demos, cross-team dependency mapping, and value-stream alignment are established practice.

the_case.need: Mentor Scrum Masters and team leaders across the organization
the_case.answer: Mentored junior Scrum Masters at Eptura. Built customized Agile training packages at Paperless Post. Facilitated org-wide Agile mentorship at Eptura through global coaching sessions.
the_case.detail: Mentoring SMs is different from coaching delivery teams. It requires modeling the discipline you want to see — which means your own practice has to be sharp. At Eptura I ran a structured SM mentorship program alongside the team-level coaching work.

the_case.need: PI Planning, ART events, dependency mapping, value-stream alignment
the_case.answer: Direct PI Planning and ART coordination at Eptura and DigitasLBI. Cross-team dependency mapping and value-stream alignment in both SAFe environments.
the_case.detail: PI Planning isn't just a meeting I've attended — it's a cadence I've led. At Eptura I coordinated PI Planning for ~10 distributed teams, managed ART-level dependencies, and maintained value-stream alignment across the program. At DigitasLBI I coordinated PI-aligned delivery in a regulated environment with offshore components.

the_case.answer: Direct ADO experience at Understood.org (2019–2020), Eptura (2022–2023), and Crocs (2026). Board configuration, workflow management, sprint tracking, delivery dashboards at program scale.
the_case.detail: ADO is my primary enterprise delivery tool. At Eptura I was the ADO configuration owner for the PMO — standardizing workflows, dashboards, and sprint tracking across ~10 teams. At Crocs I used ADO for release readiness and change control tracking.

the_case.need: Drive organizational change and continuous improvement at scale
the_case.answer: Spearheaded Agile adoption across the Marketing department at Eptura (zero prior Agile practice). Led cultural transformation at A&E (manual tracking to structured Jira delivery across ~10 teams). Sold Scrum from scratch at Underline.
the_case.detail: Organizational change at scale requires sustained coaching pressure — not a one-time workshop. At Eptura I drove Agile adoption in a department that had actively resisted it, and sustained the change through ongoing coaching, metrics visibility, and process standardization.

the_stack.headline: The scaled delivery ecosystem.
the_stack.subhead: How I coordinate, coach, and report across multiple teams simultaneously.

nodes.label: SAFe / PI Planning
nodes.desc: Scaled Agile Framework: Program Increment planning, ART coordination, system demos, cross-team alignment
nodes.pm_role: Lead or support PI Planning execution; facilitate ART syncs and Scrum-of-Scrums; coordinate cross-team dependencies and value-stream alignment.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps (ADO)
nodes.desc: Enterprise delivery tracking — board configuration, sprint tracking, delivery health dashboards
nodes.pm_role: Configure and maintain ADO board structure across teams; build leadership dashboards; track release train dependencies and program-level metrics.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.desc: Team-level delivery tracking — epics, stories, sprint boards, burndown, velocity
nodes.pm_role: Sprint board management, backlog grooming support, and AI-assisted reporting for sprint outcomes and retrospective themes.

nodes.label: Scrum-of-Scrums / ART Syncs
nodes.desc: Cross-team coordination cadence for dependency management and horizontal alignment
nodes.pm_role: Facilitate Scrum-of-Scrums across 3–10 teams; surface cross-team blockers and dependencies before they impact delivery; maintain horizontal alignment across programs.

nodes.label: Microsoft Copilot
nodes.desc: AI-assisted stakeholder reporting, meeting synthesis, and administrative overhead reduction
nodes.pm_role: Reduces time on status reporting and summary documentation, freeing capacity for coaching, facilitation, and cross-team coordination.

when_things_break.scenario: Two teams in the same ART have conflicting sprint commitments to the same stakeholder
response: Convene an emergency ART sync — not the next scheduled one
Map the conflicting commitments with dates, owners, and dependency chain
Facilitate the prioritization decision with the Product Manager — don't make it for them
Update the dependency map and communicate the resolution to all affected teams
Retrospective: how did conflicting commitments enter the program without being caught?

when_things_break.scenario: A team's Agile maturity is regressing — ceremonies declining in quality after initial improvement
response: Identify the trigger: personnel change, scope pressure, or structural issue?
Resume more intensive coaching cadence — more frequent 1:1s, ceremony observation
Re-establish working agreements explicitly — what the team committed to, what's slipping
Bring metrics into the conversation: 'Here's what the data shows has changed'
Surface to leadership if structural factors (workload, resourcing) are driving the regression

when_things_break.scenario: PI objectives are at risk mid-increment — multiple teams tracking yellow
response: Call an unscheduled ART sync within 24 hours of the pattern emerging
Triage: which objectives are recoverable, which need descoping, which need escalation?
Partner with Product Management on scope trade-offs — scope/time/quality decision
Build a recovery plan with specific owners and dates; publish to leadership immediately
Run a PI retrospective supplement to identify what planning assumptions were wrong

how_i_run_a_room: PI Planning produces committed PI objectives with risk clearly identified — not aspirational lists
ART syncs surface cross-team blockers before they stall delivery — not after
Scrum-of-Scrums has a standing dependency map that's updated before every meeting
Retrospectives produce measurable workflow changes, tracked sprint-over-sprint
Leadership reporting is evidence-based: metrics, not narratives
SM mentoring is structured and consistent — not ad hoc coaching
Organizational change is managed as a program, not a series of workshops

track_record.role: Agile Coach & Senior Scrum Master — Enterprise PMO
points: PI-aligned delivery across ~10 distributed teams (India and California)
Facilitated Scrum-of-Scrums; managed cross-team dependencies and value-stream alignment
Spearheaded Agile adoption in Marketing; mentored junior SMs org-wide
35%+ delivery efficiency gain; 20% sprint carryover reduction
Facilitated Scrum-of-Scrums across ~6 teams; 10+ concurrent SVOD workstreams
Led organizational transformation: manual tracking to structured Jira delivery
Coached Product Owners on backlog discipline and roadmap alignment
Shipped all platforms against high-visibility, externally committed launch windows
SAFe, PI-aligned delivery in a SOX-compliant financial-services environment
Coordinated PI planning, ART dependencies, and release timing across 2 teams
Coached teams on Lean-Agile practices and SAFe application
Offshore QA coordination (Costa Rica) with time-zone complexity
NEXUS Scrum coaching across iOS and mobile-web teams (8–24 combined)
Built customized Agile training packages; mentored POs on story quality
Standardized Jira/Confluence configuration across both teams

before: PI Planning produces commitments that teams don't actually believe in
Cross-team dependencies are discovered mid-sprint — after they've already created blockers
Junior SMs are running ceremonies but not coaching — the maturity ceiling is low
Organizational change initiatives stall because there's no sustained coaching behind them
after: PI objectives are realistic, risk-identified, and owned — not aspirational
Dependencies are mapped before PI Planning ends and tracked through every ART sync
Junior SMs have a structured mentorship cadence that raises the quality floor across the program
Organizational change sticks because coaching is continuous — not event-driven

the_ramp.deliverable: Meet assigned teams, SMs, POs, and key stakeholders. Review current ART and PI artifacts. Map existing dependency landscape.
Facilitate first full ceremony cycle. Attend ART sync and Scrum-of-Scrums as participant. Identify top 3 coaching priorities per team.
Full facilitation ownership. SM mentoring cadence established. First leadership metrics report published.
Running cleanly across all assigned teams. PI Planning contribution confirmed. Leadership has evidence-based team health picture.
